Linear Models Report — Experiment 3 Sub-1 Run 1

Generated 2026-04-08 12:27  |  Train: 2021–2024  |  Test: 2025
Purpose: Evaluate three linear models — OLS (unregularised baseline), Ridge (L2), and ElasticNet (L1+L2) — on Experiment 3 Sub-1 datasets (Exp2 Sub-2 baseline + ADD-tier candidate features per target). OLS is included deliberately to demonstrate where regularisation provides meaningful value and where collinearity or overfitting degradation becomes apparent. All feature sets use StandardScaler. Ridge α and ElasticNet α/l1_ratio are tuned via GridSearchCV + TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=3).
Selection criterion for “best model” cards: Win count — the number of individual datasets on which a model achieves the highest Test R² (ties go to the first model that reaches the maximum). Average Test R² is shown separately but not used for selection, because OLS produces catastrophically negative R² values on some datasets (e.g. −13.5 for Comp TSS) that would unfairly distort its mean without reflecting its typical performance.

Exp3-S1 run 1

Feature count: 20  |  Datasets: 8  |  Train: 2021–2024  |  Test: 2025

Best model: ElNet wins on Test R² in 5/8 datasets in this experiment.
Average Test R²: OLS -0.297 | Ridge +0.039 | ElNet +0.138.
Generalisation gaps: OLS shows high overfit (avg gap +1.04); Ridge shows high overfit (avg gap +0.66); ElNet shows high overfit (avg gap +0.37).
